title: clematis recta subsp. kirilowii
intro: Clematis recta subsp. kirilowii, also known as Clematis tangutica, is a flowering plant species in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is used to treat a variety of health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Clematis recta subsp. kirilowii include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and acting as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and processing the roots, leaves, or flowers for use in teas, infusions, and tinctures.
